description: This thesis proposes stream-based HW/SW co-design for memory systems in cyber-physical platforms. It introduces three architectures: InterStellar (stream-aware DRAM controller with intelligent batching and page management), InterStellarRT (real-time batches enabling tight worst-case latency bounds), and COMPASS (coordinated prefetcher and memory controller). Evaluated on 8-core RISC-V with DDR4, InterStellar achieves up to 2.72x speedup and 60% energy reduction; InterStellarRT reduces worst-case memory latency by 3.8x in isolation and 13.5x under interference, while improving bandwidth by 1.9x. The work requires no ISA or OS changes.
